# Every Role Births with a Complete Work Packet

**Flexibility: Required**

## The belief

No role operates until it has a **complete work packet** — its mandate, tools,
knowledge, data, credentials, channels, handbook (system prompt for an agent;
the "how we do things here" for a human), SOPs, KPIs, and context (the *why*).
A role is not "created" when its identity exists; it is created when it can
actually do the job on day one, the way a new hire is handed everything they
need at onboarding.

## Why we believe this

Roles launched half-equipped fail in predictable, expensive ways: an agent with
credentials but no SOPs improvises; a human hire with no KPIs drifts; either
without context makes locally-sensible, globally-wrong calls. Assembling the
packet up front is cheaper than diagnosing the failures of an under-equipped
role. "Complete" is bounded by **minimum-viable for this seat** (per JOS-P09
Start-Somewhere and JOS-P11 Only-What-Matters) — completeness means every
needed slot is filled or explicitly marked N/A, not that the packet is maximal.

## What this looks like in practice

- A role's work packet is a checklist with explicit slots; birth is gated on
  every slot being present or marked N/A-for-this-seat.
- Humans and agents fill out the *same* work-packet schema (per JOS-P27).
- The packet is assembled from discoverable, composable substrate (JOS-C49 /
  JOS-C51) — SOPs and tools come off the shelf where they exist.

## What this rules out

- Birthing an agent with tools/credentials but no SOPs, KPIs, or context.
- Onboarding a person with no packet ("they'll figure it out").
- Treating the work packet as documentation produced *after* the role is live.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C53]** Agents in an Org Chart (founds this Principle).
- **[JOS-C49]** Components as Parts on a Shelf.
- **[JOS-P25]** Design Roles by the Best-Human Question (produces what goes in the packet).
- **[JOS-P27]** Agents and Humans Are the Same Kind of Role (one packet schema for both).
- **[JOS-P09]** Start Somewhere, Iterate; **[JOS-P11]** Only What Matters (bound "complete").
